title: storage-v3 #2: repoint read path at refs/jjf/*
body:
# Goal

Repoint jjforge's read path at the v3 ref layout (per-issue refs
under `refs/jjf/issues/<id>` and `refs/jjf/memories/<key>`).

End state: `read_record`, `read_comments`, `list_records`,
`replay_ops`, and `history::read_history_at` all source their
data from v3 refs when v3 refs exist, falling back to v2 bookmark
reads when only v2 data is present. The debug-build op-replay
cross-check still runs and still catches divergence.

# Why now

Ticket 2 of 8 in the v3 storage epic (`bd98097`). Depends on
ticket 1 (the v3 write path) — once writes land on the new refs,
the reads need to find them there.

# Approach

Per the design doc's "Read path" section:

**Single-issue read** — `Storage::read_record(id)`:

1. Find the ref: `refs/jjf/issues/<id>`.
2. `git cat-file blob refs/jjf/issues/<id>:issue.json` → parse
   JSON to `Record`.

**Comments** — `Storage::read_comments(id)`:

1. `git cat-file blob refs/jjf/issues/<id>:comments.jsonl` →
   parse JSONL.
2. Handle "tree has no comments.jsonl entry" cleanly (empty
   issue) without erroring.

**Bulk read** — `Storage::list_records()`:

1. `git for-each-ref --format='%(refname) %(objectname)'
   refs/jjf/issues/`.
2. For each ref, `git cat-file blob <ref>:issue.json`.

Eventually optimizable with `git cat-file --batch` but the
naive shape is fine for this ticket.

**Op-log replay** — `replay_ops` / `history::read_history_at`:

- Walk the per-issue ref's commit chain (`git log
  refs/jjf/issues/<id> --format=%H%n%B`), parse each commit
  message for `Jjf-Op:` trailer stanzas, sort by the existing
  LWW tuple (`Jjf-At` if Some else commit_time, commit_hash,
  trailer_index), replay.
- The trailer parser (`crates/jjf-storage/src/trailer.rs`)
  is unchanged.

**Memories** — same shape as issues, under `refs/jjf/memories/<key>`.

# Scope

In scope:
- New read helpers in `crates/jjf-storage/src/git.rs` (from
  ticket 1) — `cat_file`, `for_each_ref`, `walk_commits`.
- Repoint `read_record`, `read_comments`, `list_records`,
  `replay_ops`, `history::read_history_at` at the v3 ref
  layout when v3 refs are detected.
- Preserve v2-bookmark reads when only v2 data is present
  (until ticket 4's migrator runs).
- Debug-build op-replay cross-check ports to v3 — walk the
  per-issue ref's chain instead of the bookmark's chain.

Out of scope:
- The snapshot cache (ticket 6).
- Performance optimizations (e.g. `git cat-file --batch`).
- v2 → v3 auto-migration (ticket 4).
- The actual list-ready / list-memories higher-level verbs
  (they sit on top of the basic read path and inherit the
  repointing for free).

# Acceptance

- [ ] `read_record(id)` returns the same `Record` from a v3
      ref as it does from the v2 bookmark for a freshly-created
      issue.
- [ ] `read_comments(id)` returns the same comments from a v3
      ref as it does from the v2 bookmark.
- [ ] `list_records()` enumerates every issue stored as a v3
      ref.
- [ ] Op-log replay walks the per-issue ref chain and produces
      the same `Record` as the snapshot file at the ref's tip.
- [ ] Debug-build op-replay cross-check panics on injected
      divergence in a v3-shape repo (test that today asserts
      this in v2 keeps asserting it in v3).
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green.

# Pointers

- Design doc: `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md` ("Read path"
  section).
- Code to repoint: `crates/jjf-storage/src/read.rs:118–148`
  and the `replay_ops` impl,
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/history.rs::read_history_at`.
- New module from ticket 1: `crates/jjf-storage/src/git.rs`
  (write helpers; this ticket adds the read helpers).
- Prior art: git-bug's read path; commits at
  `refs/bugs/<id>` in this repo show the per-ref-chain shape.

# Out-of-scope reminder

Do NOT change the trailer parser. The trailer block on a v3
commit message is byte-for-byte identical to a v2 commit's
trailer block — only the location of the commit changes.
